title: P3-LR-SYS: lit-review — lifecycle cost, systems benchmarking, storage/IO economics (Programme-3 Phase-0)
description: s55r Phase-0 [LIT], feeds P3-D-HW + P3-D-LIFE + P3-D-INDEX (cost-rig co-input). Deliverable reports/lit-p3-sys.md, epistemic-tagged, refs verified at source. Scope (doc S5): MLPerf inference scenarios + power methodology (implementable on our HW); latency-percentile/throughput/TTFT practice; warm/cold-cache + startup accounting; hardware repeatability (run-to-run, day-to-day variance control); storage+network IO economics; inference-volume-dependent optimal sizing (verify Sardana et al. at source); energy measurement (RAPL/NVML) pitfalls. Routed to general-purpose+web 2026-07-19. Review-gate before load-bearing.
notes: DONE 2026-07-19 (reports/lit-p3-sys.md, committed d8cee87e). Source-verification pass (prior Fable draft docs/next/lit/SYS.md 07-11 exists). KEY findings: energy-measurement gaming/repro risk -> boundary-as-first-class + system-energy-UNMEASURED + unmeasured-domain-wins-UNPROVEN; open-loop Poisson (coordinated omission); repeatability-as-GATE ([STIPULATED] band); MLPerf=discipline-not-instrument. FLAG: Sardana $-figures FAILED cross-verify vs the 07-11 draft -> [claimed], needs direct-PDF read before KOT-LIFE cites them (reconciliation follow-up). Feeds P3-D-HW/P3-D-LIFE/P3-D-INDEX cost-rig.
close_reason: Phase-0 lit-review DELIVERABLE done + committed (reports/lit-p3-*.md, source-verified, divergences caught). Review-gate applies to the consuming Phase-1 designs, not the lit-review itself.

Feeds P3-D-INDEX (consolidate with P3-MF-0 + P3-LR-SYS at the KOT-FAIR/2 freeze). Review-gate + Fable before load-bearing; the composite-index decision surfaces at P3-D-INDEX.

title: P3-MF-0: draft the frozen KOT-FAIR/2 measurement-framework spec artifact (Programme-3 Phase-0 gating)
description: s55r Phase-0 gating deliverable (parallel, no cross-deps per doc S5). Draft the concrete frozen KOT-FAIR/2 spec per docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md S2.4: threat model (S2.0); KOT-AI-INDEX/2 suite pins + domain structure + normalisation (S1.1); KOT-SIZE/2 packing recipe + frozen base-image hash (S1.2); KOT-COST/2 rig + warm/cold + HW-repeatability (S1.3); KOT-LIFE/1 ledger template (S1.3a); decontamination screener; sealed-eval protocol; frontier-builder + comparator-pinning rule; stat analysis plan wiring (S2.5). Plus the calibration-report PROTOCOL (135M/360M/1.7B pure-neural, index separates known-ordered models + cost-rig day/reboot repeatability — instrument validated on known answers before judging any neurosymbolic system). SPEC design routed to GPT-5.6 2026-07-19; calibration RUNS (~$0 local+free-credits) are the execution follow-up. Gates P3-D-INDEX (KOT-FAIR/2 freeze). Review-gate + Fable critique before freeze.
notes: REVIEW-GATE DONE 2026-07-19 (docs/next/design/kot-fair-2-review1-freeze-readiness.md; source poc/gpt56-review/p3mf0-review-reconcile/). VERDICT: NOT freeze-ready. Definite defects: floor rule BACKWARD (UCB->LCB); parent factorial-attribution controls MISSING from draft; 3x ceilings endogenous; energy boundary unsound. 12 ordered reconciliation edits (Part B) + 10 freeze-reqs. PART C correctness-instrument gap: framework reports-but-cannot-adjudicate a correctness win; needs a correctness endpoint family; how to incorporate (2nd-claim/gate/Pareto-axis) = MAINTAINER strategic decision (ties to #57 crux). NEXT: maintainer/Fable resolve the correctness-instrument re-weighting -> apply the 12 edits (Fable/careful pass) -> re-review -> P3-D-INDEX freeze. Framework refinement gated on the strategic decision.

title: FABLE (first-wave PRIORITY): optimal-resource-usage plan — work taxonomy + architecture reuse + cross-experiment GPU-result reuse
description:
Maintainer-designated PRIORITY (Jesse, 2026-07-09): when Fable is back online, one of its FIRST tasks is to produce a concrete OPTIMAL-RESOURCE-USAGE plan (deliverable: docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md + integration into the research-engine framework + roles/practices). Do this EARLY — before large new experiment spend — because it optimizes all downstream work. Three required components:

(1) WORK TAXONOMY (Opus vs Fable boundary, made precise). Define, with a concrete decision rubric, exactly which work is ROUTINE / non-complex and can be done by cheaper models (Opus execution) WITHOUT reducing the quality or potential of experiments, vs which is complex/creative/definitional and must stay Fable. Refine the existing Fable-designs / Opus-runs split (docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md, research-engine.md §3, .claude/agents/experiment-runner.md + kb-pipeline-runner.md) into a checklist/flowchart an agent can apply per task. Goal: maximise cheap-model throughput with zero quality loss; flag any grey zones as forks.

(2) ARCHITECTURE REUSE + COMPOSITION. Identify where architectures (kernel / encoder / model components) can be RE-USED and PLUGGED TOGETHER in different combinations to test architectural permutations efficiently — where the existing work to build ONE kernel can be reused to build a SIMILAR kernel (e.g. shared basis, shared minting, shared corpora, shared adapters), and where components compose so a permutation grid is covered by a small set of reusable building blocks rather than N bespoke builds. Connect to the idea-registry slots/permutations (registry/ideas.jsonl), the architecture ladder (docs/next/architecture-ladder.md), and the kernel-variant expansion split.

(3) CROSS-EXPERIMENT GPU-RESULT REUSE. Identify where LOGGED training/eval outputs from one GPU run can be REUSED across multiple experiments/analyses instead of re-running — i.e. bake in the 're-analyse before re-running' principle (the F2 -> f2b lesson: gap-closure was a free re-analysis over logged data). Define a results/artifact ledger + reuse rules: which arms/checkpoints/decodes are reusable across experiments, and a gate that forces a check for an existing logged result before authorising any new GPU spend.

Output: the plan doc + concrete mechanisms (rubric, reusable-component map, results-reuse ledger + pre-spend check) folded into the engine so they are enforced, not advisory.
notes:
REFINEMENT (Jesse, 2026-07-09): do NOT frame components (2) architecture-reuse and (3) GPU-result-reuse as retroactive gates ONLY. ALSO add a PROACTIVE component (4): produce a reuse-MAXIMISING ORDERING/prioritisation of pending work — sequence experiments so broadly-reusable artefacts are produced first and shared arms/checkpoints/corpora are built once and consumed many times, rather than only checking for reuse at spend time. Opus has already done the PLANNING-STEP-NOW reconnaissance the maintainer requested: docs/next/reuse-ordering-analysis.md (SPECULATIVE/uncommitted-then-committed; Opus recon, NOT a decision) — it maps dependency edges, shared pins, logged GPU outputs, and proposes a draft wave ordering (Wave 1 zero-GPU: l3a-parse/a5-nl/g5/g4+build d-ax; then POST-F2-INFRA decision; then e9-full→e9-c shared-producer-first; efficiency f-track; f7 last consuming survivors' decodes; family-h0→a-h0). Fable OWNS validating/rejecting/re-deriving this ordering and every 'reuse is licensed' call — esp. the load-bearing honesty question (§5): whether pre-registration discipline permits an experiment to consume another record's logged per-item decodes as its own arm output WITHOUT a fresh run (f2b was a replication, not pure reuse). Decide whether this folds into the backlog L-score (research-engine.md §2.7) or stands as a separate ordering.
REUSE-PERMISSION DEFERRED (maintainer, 2026-07-09). Two cross-vendor Codex audits of the confirmatory logged-result-reuse ruling: audit-1 CONFIRM_WITH_CONDITIONS (2 criticals), revision-1 closed RC-8/RC-5/ASM, re-audit-2 REFUTE (4 criticals remained, enforcement weaker than prose; RC-7 cosmetic-in-code). Maintainer chose DEFER over revision-2: at ~$1/GPU-run the ~$1/run saving does not justify the integrity surface. LIVE: restrictive gate + fail-closed interlock + reuse_overrides + exploratory-quarantine. DEFERRED: reused_from consumption (fail-closed, no ratification file). Plan components (1)(2)(4) unaffected. Audit trail: registry/audits/resource-optimization-plan/{1-design-audit-codex,2-design-reaudit-codex}.json. This closes the P0 plan deliverable EXCEPT the deferred reuse sub-feature.

title: Kernel construction methodology: how should canonical concept representations actually be built?
description: @jeswr (2026-07-07) stepped back from the identity/reconciliation question to the FOUNDATIONAL one: is LLM-generation (Haiku emitting NSM explications) even the right way to construct the kernel? Wikipedia-sourcing was 'a whim'. Candidate alternatives to research + test: (a) LLM-generated NSM explications (current); (b) DICTIONARY-GRAPH + convergence — turn each dictionary definition into relations, run a convergence algorithm to a fixpoint (mirrors the published 'grounding kernel' / minimum-grounding-set work of Vincent-Lamarre/Masse/Harnad 'The Latent Structure of Dictionaries', and Formal Concept Analysis); (c) EXTRACT FROM MODEL INTERNALS — 'deconstruct a local model so its output layer matches the construction we want' (SAE/dictionary-learning/interp; connects to this project's E8 kernel<->SAE alignment result); (d) principled hybrids. Concern: an LLM 'just spits out what it thinks things should be like' — may be circular/ungrounded/non-canonical.
design: Deep-research workflow w7tu4h3kr (5 Fable angles + synthesis): decompositional-semantics theory (is a canonical primitive basis real? NSM vs Fodor's atomism); dictionary-graphs/grounding-kernels (Harnad et al; FCA); symbol-grounding/circularity (Harnad SGP; distributional vs decompositional; is a definition-only kernel grounded?); model-internal extraction (SAE/monosemanticity/linear-rep; canonical? deterministic? training-free?); LLM-generator critique (proposer-then-verified vs source-of-truth; determinism from a stochastic generator). Deliverable: theory-grounded recommendation + honest uncertainty map + a SEQUENCED experimental programme (cheapest/most-decision-relevant first) to empirically discriminate the approaches. E10-fix (order-invariance of reconciliation) becomes ONE rung, downstream of choosing the substrate.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Theory-grounded recommended construction approach (or principled hybrid) with reasoning. 2) Explicit uncertainty map (settled-by-literature vs open-empirical vs philosophical). 3) A proper sequenced set of experiments, each with question/method/pass-fail/cost/decision-unblocked. 4) Clear verdict on whether LLM-generation is legitimate as a verified-proposer vs circular as source-of-truth.
notes: REVIEW-GATE DONE 2026-07-19 (poc/gpt56-review/99a-proposal-review/last-message.json): verdict NEEDS-FIX — send to Fable as a draft, NOT to maintainer as the recommended path. Reframing (semantic-record-first) SOUND, retain. Minimum fixes: (1) operationalise independent-endorsement + separate canonical/evidence-adequate/grounded + soften criterion-4 reproducibility; (2) DEMOTE graph-constraint recommendation->pre-registered HYPOTHESIS (K-NULL plain-text-equivalent-cheaper dominates its own objection); (3) KBUILD-0 add citation-only-no-graph arm (decisive ablation) + human-from-evidence arm + PACKET-IDENTIFIABILITY gate + template-leakage audits, make exact construction-fidelity PRIMARY (host-BA secondary), fix stats (8-across-3 impossible, N/T need CIs, coverage evaluator-vs-constructor, selection bias) + precedence matrix. [SV] lit non-load-bearing. NEXT: revise (Fable/deliberate-cycle) -> Fable critique -> lit-verify [SV] -> THEN maintainer. NOT surfaced to maintainer (held per review verdict). Proposal+review are a solid draft — natural stopping point; revision is a substantial redesign best for Fable.

title: F1-K bring-up timing-step fails: engine emits [score N req|Xs timer only at 5-req boundaries; per-sid runs score <5 -> die
description: Bring-up on kot-f1k-run STOPped rc=1 at step 5 (timing) 2026-07-19T04:31. Root cause: worker (poc/gcp/f1k_worker.sh:1621-1627) greps stderr for '[score N req | X.Ys' and dies if absent; the GLM engine (glm_moe_dsa) emits that timer line ONLY at 5-request boundaries (verified: func-patched-1.err has '[score 5 req | 854.6s', '[score 10 req | 1682.6s', '[score 15 req | 3902.7s'), but the per-sid timing gate runs score <5 requests each (gate-run-t1-s001.out = 1 result) -> no timer line -> ERR_F1K_WORKER die. The FUNCTIONAL GATE PASSED (determinism + inertness/pristine-vs-KaE byte-identity — addresses 2zh8 #5). Model placement is FINE (ext4 Local-SSD RAID0 /dev/md0 749.7G; the engine's '9p/Windows filesystem' warning is a spurious FS-detection false-positive on striped md0 — do NOT re-stage). MATERIAL THROUGHPUT FINDING: measured ~170-305s/prefill (real CPU-bound avx2, matches probe compute-bound-long caveat) — feeds affordability. FIX (harness, spend-relevant): adapt the timing to the engine's 5-req-boundary timer (batch >=5 timing texts per engine load so >=1 timer fires; extract load-excluded per-prefill time from the boundary deltas), OR implement the design-note fallback (1566: 'NULL blended => runner measures on-box') if the downstream f1k_gcp.py affordability truly measures on-box. Then re-run timing on the live VM (estate ready) -> affordability GREEN/STOP. Blocks the bring-up affordability verdict.
notes: FIX COMMITTED f40112a9 (TIMER_REP correctness fix; f1k_worker.sh only). Agent confirmed engine timer semantics from source (colibri glm.c run_score): load-EXCLUDED, cumulative, 5-req-boundary only. MEASURED unpinned load-excluded per-prefill: short 168s / long 447s / blended 307.6s. Affordability: unpinned BUSTS frozen window [260.6,900]h (blended->1706h/$273, short->933h); GO case rests on UNMEASURED pinned+concept-batched rate (~83s target; 07-17 probe 0.76 hit suggests plausible). Bring-up INCONCLUSIVE on affordability. Surfaced to maintainer as github #55 (4 decisions: pinned-measurement approach [batched-distinct redesign vs TIMER_REP+timeout vs small probe]; representativeness warm-repeat-vs-concept-batch [Fable]; GO/NO-GO stance [rec: no spend until pinned rate confirms]; VM/estate disposition). Functional gate PASSED (inertness proven, nf5n closed). Bead stays OPEN pending the #55 decisions + the decisive pinned measurement.

title: glm-s4drop-0 round-4 refreeze: address GPT-5.6 NEEDS-FIX (io-digest byte-contract + independent recompute + ASM/naming)
description: GPT-5.6 GO-FOR-REREVIEW of the round-3 frozen glm-s4drop-0 returned NEEDS-FIX (verdict poc/gpt56-review/s4drop-round3-rereview/last-message.json, 2026-07-19). Statistical design + freeze legitimacy AGREE (frozen hash recomputes c5cc6d02, corrections genuinely pre-run, controls/endpoint/power/kill/envelope sound). BLOCKS the glm-s4drop-0 GPU run until a round-4 refreeze lands these 6 pre-run correction/refreeze items (NOT ops-amendment): (1) DESIGN: define a canonical kot-s4traj/1 digest profile — exact byte encoding (domain tag + count + ordered fixed-width token IDs; endianness; framing; prompt-ID inclusion) — currently 'SHA-256 over the exact forced token-id sequence' is undefined so two impls can diverge; (2) ENG: emit the actual forced token IDs (or an independently-consumable per-arm replay trace) and have the pinned analysis RECOMPUTE both row digests and the reference map from the pinned reference artifact (today analysis only syntactically checks 64-hex; a runner could copy the expected digest while replaying different IDs and pass — analysis:45-53,393-419); (3) ENG: add a self-test supplying equal-length DIVERGENT token arrays while copying the expected/report digest — must FAIL via independent recomputation (today's adversarial test only mutates the digest string — analysis:1383-1393); (4) ENG: implement + pin the production replay/digest runner (stage4 dir currently has mask/suite/mock, PINNED-AT-INPUTS — digest-byte contract unverifiable against production code); (5) MECH: rename round-3 rows consistently kot-s4row/2 -> /3 in seq-2 correction (analyzer already requires /3); (6) MECH: renumber the colliding stage-4 ASM-2401..2403 (reused from retired molecule-S5) to globally-unique IDs + update/refreeze every reference. Design item (1) routable to GPT-5.6 (Fable down) then coordinator-synthesise; items 2-6 engineering+refreeze. Spawned from hu7n on NEEDS-FIX.
notes: DIGEST-PROFILE DESIGN LANDED 2026-07-19 (GPT-5.6, poc/gpt56-review/s4traj-digest-design/last-message.json) — resolves re-review items 1-3 BY DESIGN. kot-s4traj/1 profile: SHA256 preimage = ASCII('kot-s4traj/1\n') || U32BE(len(S))||S || U32BE(len(P))||P || U32BE(len(A))||A || U64BE(N) || U32BE(T[0..N-1]) where S=row-schema(bump to kot-s4row/4), P=prompt_id, A=arm, T=actual forced-replay token IDs in POSITION order (NOT numeric sort), N derived from the trace (not trusted from forced_tokens); domain-separated, length-framed, EOF terminal, unsigned-32bit ids. Conformance vector digest eefda751... (arm-binding: A=s4-b0 gives 2c3adaf4...). Reference map -> arm-indexed {arm:{prompt_id:digest}}, recomputed from the PIN-VERIFIED raw reference artifact (hash bytes + match meta.pins.reference_trajectories, recompute whole map; reported map audit-only). 8-step independent-recompute contract (analysis recomputes row + reference digests, io_match_valid=false on any mismatch; never trusts reported strings). Adversarial oracle: swap-last-2-IDs fixture, copy H(E) into traj_sha256 -> io_match_valid=false/all_valid=false/pass_gate=false. 3 COORDINATOR DECISIONS flagged: (a) freeze kot-s4row/4 (/3->/4 material schema bump adding forced_token_ids); (b) how the pinned reference artifact reaches the stdin-only analyzer (actual bytes / authenticated trace, not just reported digest); (c) trajectory boundary (continuation IDs per forced_tokens, exclude prompt/context; BOS/EOS only if in the counted buffer). REMAINING: review-gate this design -> ratify (a-c) -> implement items 2-4 (row emits forced_token_ids; analysis recompute; runner emits+hashes the exact forced buffer) -> mech items 5-6 (kot-s4row/2->/3->/4 naming; ASM-2401..2403 renumber) -> round-4 refreeze. Low-urgency (glm-s4drop-0 run is GPU-gated).

title: F1-K bring-up: objdump inertness REFERENCE flags (-O2 -march=x86-64-v3) SPILL on the VM gcc too -> Fable design decision (move reference pass off-box-only per memo residual-risk #3)
description:
OWNER: Fable (FROZEN-GATE DESIGN decision; NOT an ops retry). Blocks the F1-K on-box bring-up. The ops implementation is ALREADY COMMITTED (4c9af0d2, coordinator) and RAN; this bead is the DESIGN follow-up its outcome forces.

CONSEQUENCE: the fail-closed reference objdump pass precedes the memo's new AUTHORITATIVE functional inert-by-default gate in f1k_worker.sh, so the functional byte-identity gate (the whole point of the resolution) NEVER RAN (nor advisory-native, nor glm_pristine build; step 5 exited 1 first). MEMO-NAMED RESOLUTION (residual-risk #3, Fable to decide/freeze): 'move the reference pass off-box-only, not widening the set' — keep the fail-closed reference-flags objdump as the $0 OFF-BOX battery (control box / real-checks.sh, already at x86-64-v3) and let on-box bring-up run ONLY the AUTHORITATIVE functional KAE-unset byte-identity gate (+ advisory native objdump for audit). Options for Fable: (1) reference pass off-box-only [memo-preferred]; (2) on-box objdump advisory-only (functional gate authoritative); (3) other. All are ops-harness edits to bringup_gcp.sh/f1k_worker.sh (still NO re-freeze; frozen f1k.json 505165ee carries no objdump obligation) — but the CHOICE is Fable design. Runner must NOT improvise (relocating/removing a fail-closed gate = design).

ASM-2503 ALREADY REGISTERED (MEASURED, in registry/assumptions.jsonl via 4c9af0d2) but PREDATES this datum: it describes the native spill + control-box/x86-64-v3 PASS. It NEEDS A CORRECTIVE APPEND (register is append-only; last line for an id is current) adding: 'the reference flags -O2 -march=x86-64-v3 ALSO spill on the VM Ubuntu-22.04 gcc (same [attention,run_serve,tok_load]+outlined set); the x86-64-v3 PASS holds only on control-box gcc 11.5 + znver2/znver3, NOT the VM gcc.' READY TO EXECUTE ON DECISION: functional gate + determinism pre-check + advisory native objdump + glm_pristine build are implemented, committed (4c9af0d2), and mock-green. Once Fable frees the on-box objdump reference pass, the runner re-launches (est ~$0.05 staging + ~2-3h functional gate on the 384GB model), no further design work. VM torn down, zero orphans, ~$0.05 spent this pass.

title: F1-K compute-target vs frozen cost-model MISMATCH: Modal (~$1.15/h) infeasible under pinned analysis (needs <=$0.595/h effective; frozen target = AWS spot i4i.2xlarge @ $0.28/h)
description: OPUS runner-3 fail-closed pre-spend STOP on bead xq0h (NO paid spend; all $0 gates GREEN). BLOCKER: the frozen F1-K cost model + the pinned analysis (analysis/f1k.py sha 54924cfd) are designed for AWS SPOT i4i.2xlarge @ $0.20-0.28/h and are INCOMPATIBLE with the mandated Modal compute (repo-measured $1.15/h, poc/modal/modal_glm52_stage3.py RATE_PER_HR). ARITHMETIC [MEASURED from frozen constants]: analysis hard-rejects (ERR_P2_ANALYSIS, no verdict) any ledger with instance_hours NOT in [260.6,900] (INSTANCE_HOURS_MIN=260.6/WALL_CLOCK_CAP=900), usd_total NOT in [73,155] (USD_TOTAL_MIN=73/USD_CAP=155), or violating coherence usd_total ~= instance_hours*spot_rate (usd_spent_prior covers construction_instance_hours*rate; COST_TOL $0.01). => admissible EFFECTIVE RATE = [73/900, 155/260.6] = [$0.081, $0.595]/h. AWS spot i4i.2xlarge $0.28 is IN-window (design ASM-2205: 462.1h->$129.40, 521.2h corner->$146; glm52-f1k-cost-reduction.md 'DEFAULT for all GLM-5.2 runs'; EC2 compute only; GPU explicitly REJECTED). Modal $1.15/h is OUT-of-window: honest Modal ledger (rate 1.15, hours>=260.6) => usd_total>=$300 > $155 max => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS; report usd_total<=155 w/ hours>=260.6 => rate<=0.595!=1.15 => coherence fail; report hours<260.6 => floor fail; stop at $155 mid-run => INCOMPLETE (prefills<11011). NO honest Modal path yields a valid verdict; launching would spend up to $155 to CERTAINLY produce INSTRUMENT-INVALID. Even AWS on-demand i4i ($0.69) fails the window; only SPOT i4i.2xlarge ($0.28) closes it. DECISION NEEDED (Fable/coordinator/maintainer; NOT a runner call): (1) run F1-K on the frozen compute target = AWS spot i4i.2xlarge (~$0.28/h) + per-item checkpoint/resume (design DEFAULT) — reconcile with the task's 'NO AWS' instruction + provide i4i spot access; OR (2) a NON-AWS provider at effective rate <=$0.595/h with a 900GB-NVMe CPU box holding the 384GB int4 estate + expert pinning (Lightning ~$0.19/h and Anyscale per MEMORY compute note — must be validated vs the i4i-class disk/RAM/pinning assumptions ASM-2205 rests on, then re-priced); OR (3) Fable re-derives the F1-K cost model for the chosen provider's rate and RE-FREEZES (raise usd_cap + adjust analysis floors INSTANCE_HOURS_MIN/USD_TOTAL_MIN) — a much larger spend authorization than #46's $155. Key smoke findings: (a) §6.5 Gaussian crosstalk model HOLDS for truly-disjoint pairs (canon σ 0.01096 vs pred 0.01105); (b) margin killer is §6.5 collision-class-2 structural duplicates (86 zero-axiom adverbs → identical vectors), lexical block splits them; (c) global RDM-Spearman 0.26 vs X4's 0.9718 — does NOT transfer; top-pairs(cos>0.05) Spearman 0.977 does; strong-NN recall@1 at 512 only ~0.64. 10k run in progress (background).
close_reason: S0 first rung delivered: poc/scale pipeline (ingest→typing→vectorise→metrics), 1k smoke + full 10k run complete on 2 shared cores (~15 min total, checkpointed, byte-determinism verified at both 1k and 10k). Headline: §6.5 Gaussian crosstalk curve HOLDS (σ 0.01106 vs 0.01105 @8192); binding margin constraint is 20.1% structural-duplicate mass (§6.5 class 2), not crosstalk; X4 0.97 RDM calibration does NOT transfer to bulk import (global 0.26; strong-NN recall@1 0.41 @512); storage matches §6.4 exactly; encoding not the 1M wall (~11 CPU-h) but exact O(m²) cleanup is (324 CPU-h @1M → ANN gate mandatory). title: f2b-transfer judge-2 LLM-judge prompt + codex invocation shape NOT frozen (FABLE-NEEDED): §4 names a GPT-5.5 codex judge with a 'pinned prompt stored in d-adj-t' but no verbatim prompt/invocation exists; Opus stopped, no codex spend
description:
Opus-runner STAGE-1 PREP (run poc/f2b-transfer/opus-runs/20260709T171156Z/run-log.md). design.md sha matches the FROZEN prereg_doc pin (c4942eaf...); f2b-transfer frozen-drift ok (f00fd28f); d-qa-t corpus-pin 7179ee67...cee27 verified. Judge-1 HUMAN package assembled + committed (blind, deterministic, verbatim §4). Judge-2 (GPT-5.5 fallback) is BOUNDARY-STOPPED.

FABLE-NEEDED: the §4 LLM-judge (judge-2) mechanism is NOT fully determined by the frozen protocol, so per the Opus task's explicit guard and the Fable/Opus boundary (authoring instruction/context prompts for a judging agent is Fable design work), Opus did NOT invent it and did NOT spend on codex. What is missing:

1. The judge-2 "pinned prompt". design.md §4.1 says judge-2 is "the cross-vendor Codex/GPT-5.5 model via the codex CLI, temperature 0, pinned prompt stored in d-adj-t, shown ONLY the item text". But d-adj-t does not exist yet, and NO verbatim prompt text exists anywhere (frozen record, design.md, or repo). The exact operative wording is load-bearing for the labels (framing "answer from your own understanding of everyday concepts"; how the model must express the mandatory cannot-say / NONE escape per §4.3; the strict output format to parse) and materially shapes the label distribution. It must FAITHFULLY encode the same §4 protocol the judge-1 human package presents so both judges adjudicate ONE instrument (needed for endorsement A and the raw two-judge agreement gate).

2. The codex invocation SHAPE. The Opus task named `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -s read-only` (E5 read-only discipline) but the frozen design does not pin: per-item vs batched calls; the exact stdin/prompt structure; how temperature-0 is enforced/verified; the machine-parseable answer contract (e.g. single token A/B/C/D/NONE or yes/no/cannot-say) and how refusals map. title: FABLE DESIGN + sign-off: two maintainer-proposed I/O-compression kernel ideas (idea-crosslingual-phrase-coverage-io-compression, idea-compositional-rollup-invented-concepts)
description:
Jesse 2026-07-09 proposed two EFFICIENCY-thesis compression ideas, registered in registry/ideas.jsonl (status: proposed, premise_eligible: false, EXTRAPOLATION). EXPLICIT: do NOT build yet — Fable must create AND sign off a decisive experiment design first (this is a Fable design task, not Opus execution).

(1) idea-crosslingual-phrase-coverage-io-compression: mint concepts for the highest-frequency words/phrases ACROSS languages (statistical selection) so the encoder compresses LLM input into few concept tokens + the decoder expresses high-bandwidth content in few tokens; generate definitions cheaply via a Haiku concept-definer with a fully CACHED prefix and only the single word as variable input (tiny, ideally symbolic, output). FORK Jesse flagged: does asking for symbolic output cut tokens or INCREASE Haiku thinking-time? cheap decisive sub-test first. Generates an ALTERNATIVE kernel.

(2) idea-compositional-rollup-invented-concepts: encoder does static/symbolic roll-up of 'a is b' / 'a AND b' / 'a OR c' structure into a single INVENTED composite concept (vector deterministically derived via the existing Hadamard+HRR binding algebra) so those tokens reach the LLM as one concept; decoder gets a per-utterance invented-concept dictionary for lossless expansion.

Design constraints for Fable: BOTH must beat the kernel-as-text null (Law 2) and survive the trained-bridge overhead (Law 1); confront the LCM/CALM fixed-semantic-IO scaling penalty (phi-fixedness axis); and — critically — idea (2) is constrained by idea-ast-input-injection-boundary (X1): frame on the COMPRESSION/EFFICIENCY endpoint, NEVER as accuracy-from-structure (which X1 showed is parity + non-transferring). Pre-register with a cost/accuracy Pareto metric vector before any spend.
notes:
MAINTAINER SIGN-OFF 2026-07-09 ('you have my sign off on all queued things'). Authorized to proceed through the normal rails (design->build->freeze/mock->run->verdict->Codex audit->Fable interpret for spending experiments). compression ideas cleared: Tier-0 censuses ($0) + A-F0 fork sub-test (~$5-10) first.

title: Order-invariant kernel reconciliation: dependency-aware re-derivation + bidirectional co-definition
description: Concepts are minted in frequency-rank order, but definitional dependency does NOT follow frequency. A concept defined early (e.g. 'male') may be expressible more concisely/faithfully once a higher-level concept (e.g. 'person') is later available as a molecule — yet there is NO reconciliation pass that revisits earlier concepts to re-express them over later-minted higher-level concepts. Consequence: the kernel's conciseness AND canonicity depend on mint order, so two corpora built in different orders would differ. That contradicts the core claim of canonical, deterministic concept vectors — canonicity requires order-invariance (a fixpoint). Raised by @jeswr 2026-07-07. DEFERRED to a Claude-Fable session (design work; no Fable access now).
design: Two coupled sub-problems. (1) Dependency-aware re-derivation: detect concepts whose explication shortens/improves by referencing a now-available higher-level molecule, and re-derive them. Content-addressing (urn:kot: = hash of explication) means re-derivation CHANGES the hash -> need either (a) versioning/migration (old->new urn, rewrite referrers) or (b) settle-to-fixpoint-THEN-freeze: iterate re-derivation on mutable IDs to convergence, content-address only once stable. (2) Bidirectional/mutually-referential cycles: buy<->sell, parent<->child, left<->right, cause<->effect, borrow<->lend — each naturally defined via the other, but rank-order forces one first (bare primes) and the other gets the concise cross-ref. Options: co-definition per strongly-connected component, or a graph fixpoint. Procedure: build dependency graph; compute SCCs; topologically re-derive across SCCs, jointly solve within each; iterate to fixpoint; THEN content-address. Ties to E9: a reconciled kernel should be MORE order-invariant; measure vector drift pre/post.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Reconciliation pass yields an order-invariant settled corpus (same fixpoint regardless of mint order; verify with two random-order subsets). 2) Bidirectional pairs handled by co-definition, not first-mover verbosity. 3) Migration/versioning story for content-addresses. 4) Measure avg explication size + order-invariance before/after.

All 18 reference-bearing concepts decode exactly; failures are the quote-re-anchored emotion trio. Synthetic X2 gate (720/720 full grid) never generates these shapes — extend the generator or add authored-corpus regression fixtures.
Root-caused (decode-spec-fix agent): SPEC RULING — the afraid RECORD IS LEGAL and the VALIDATOR IS RIGHT per gist §4.2 ('exactly one introducing occurrence'; 'a cdef:ref to an undeclared or not-yet-introduced index is a gate error'; 'Quote-local referents (bound inside a cdef:quoteClauses) scope to that quote'): ref 2 is declared, bound exactly once in quote clause 0, and every mention follows it inside the same quote. The decoder never disagreed about the RULE — it DROPPED the introducing SP (decoded HAPPEN.roles={}), then failed its own output's validation. Same mechanism as angry/sad: at quote>op depth the true optional SP's presence signature (0.0335) sits BELOW pure-noise slots at the same depth (0.036-0.040) vs the 5-sigma gate 0.0552 — locally unseparable. FIX: decoder residual-completion phase (encoder/src/decoder.ts): after convergence, re-encode the decoded AST, subtract from v; missed content is then nearly the only residual content (measured 6-30x SNR at the missed sites: head 0.35-0.67, quant 0.33-0.68, bind ref:2 0.31, temporal op 0.46 vs noise ~0.02); probe every empty optional site there and accept a candidate only if the re-encoded edited clause explains the restored residual strictly better (exact matched-filter competition; phantoms cannot win). Encoder content-hash UNCHANGED (40e8c8ba...e2d10c); corpus UNCHANGED (no re-authoring, corpus hash stays f23f5211...); unit suite 38/38 (5 new regression tests incl. both validator ruling directions, encoder/test/corpus-regression.test.ts).
close_reason: X2-corpus re-run PASS: minimal 54/54 AND full-corpus 54/54 exact (was 51/54 both; report x2-kernel-v0-report.md 2026-07-07T04:22Z). Encoder content-hash unchanged (40e8c8ba...e2d10c), corpus untouched (hash f23f5211... unchanged, no X1-corpus re-run needed), unit suite 38/38. title: Provenance integrity: 9 duplicate ASM IDs in assumptions.jsonl (last-line-wins masks a defect)
description: The GPT-5.6 glm-s4drop-0 re-review (2026-07-19) flagged ASM-2401..2403 reused between retired molecule-S5 and stage-4. A programme-wide scan of registry/assumptions.jsonl (1305 ids) finds 9 DUPLICATED ids: ASM-0010, 0011, 0023 (legacy/early), ASM-2389, 2399 (2399 may be an intentional 'revised 2399' per the glm-s4drop freeze — VERIFY), 2401, 2402, 2403 (molecule-S5-vs-stage4 — addressed in euva), 2455. JSON last-line-wins means the later claim is 'current' so live gates mostly resolve correctly, but globally reusing an id for an unrelated claim defeats stable provenance (a frozen record asserting these are uniquely-registered anchors is not clean). TO DO (coordinator, governance-careful — some may be in frozen records needing refreeze): per-id, determine whether each dup is (a) an unintended collision to renumber to a unique id + update/refreeze references, or (b) an intentional in-place revision (then the SCHEMA should mark supersession, not silently duplicate the id). 2401-2403 handled in euva; this bead covers 0010/0011/0023/2389/2399/2455. Detection: python scan grouping by 'id'.
notes: TRIAGED 2026-07-19 (all 9 dups). BENIGN intentional revisions (last-line-wins, 2nd line explicitly supersedes — schema-hygiene only, mark supersession explicitly): ASM-0010, ASM-0011 (REVISION-1 REWRITE post-Codex-audit), ASM-2389 (SUPERSEDED re-review 5c), ASM-2399 (Stage-4 decision-boundary revision D1/D2). LIKELY-BENIGN same-topic (verify exact-dup vs revision): ASM-0023 (nsk1-eval 3-gen-family). REAL COLLISIONS (unrelated claims sharing an id — genuine defects needing renumber+refreeze of the affected record): ASM-2401/2402/2403 (molecule-S5 REPILOT vs stage-4 io_match/residuals — in euva) AND ASM-2455 (molecule-S5 RE-PILOT attribution L1252 vs F1-K round-10 carrier content-auth floors L1253). ROOT PATTERN: retired molecule-S5's ASM ids (2401-2403, 2455) were REUSED by later experiments (stage-4, F1-K round-10) after the pivot freed them → collisions. FIX SCOPE: the benign revisions need only a schema-supersession marker (or leave); the 4 collisions need the LATER claim renumbered to a fresh id + refreeze of its record (2401-2403 via euva/glm-s4drop; 2455 via the F1-K round-10 carrier record). Recommend a dup-id guard in the ASM-register tooling to prevent recurrence.

title: b-cov-smol census BLOCKED: needs Fable instrument design (kappa_B over non-definitional SmolLM2-nine) before it can run
description:
Opus experiment-runner scoped b-cov-smol (docs/next/benchmark-evaluation-strategy.md sec.4 item 1) and STOPPED at the STEP-1 decision gate: it is NOT a mechanical reuse of existing tooling, so running it would require inventing coverage semantics (designer/Fable lane). No run performed, no files created, registry-check PASS.

FINDING: b-cov-smol's headline deliverable is the per-benchmark kappa_B baseline (sec.10.1) via the two BINDING gates kappa_B^verify and kappa_B^engine. Neither has a reusable operationalization for externally-authored items, and 6 of the 9 benchmarks are non-definitional. `kernel_checkable` is today a build-time AUTHORED boolean (per-corpus stipulation), never a computed predicate over arbitrary item text; the only NL->kot-query leg (mapper/src/defineTemplates.ts) is CLOSED/definitional-only and onto-obo (biomedical-label) bound. So kappa_B^engine is plausibly ==0-by-construction for HellaSwag/WinoGrande/PiQA/GSM8K/ARC-Challenge/TriviaQA/CSQA (a MEASURED-null result Fable must characterize, not Opus).

DESIGN QUESTIONS FOR FABLE (each blocks a MEASURED kappa_B cell):
1. kappa_B^verify operationalization over externally-authored items: define the predicate deciding whether a canonical record backs a decidable proposition (P10) for an arbitrary external MCQ/open item — today only ever a per-corpus build-time stipulation.
2. kappa_B^engine coverage of non-definitional items: how does expressibility-coverage apply to continuation/pronoun/affordance/arithmetic/reasoning/trivia items given the only NL->query leg is definitional+biomedical? Is kappa_B^engine ==0 by construction (report MEASURED-null) or is a new mapper leg in scope?
3. sec.C5 uniqueness-filter operationalization for non-definitional items (docs/design-kot-query-define-op.md C5 defines it only for def-MMLU-style candidate-definition items).
4. R-C lemma-touch "supporting fact" mapping for the 8 non-OpenBookQA benchmarks (only OpenBookQA has fact1; the field playing that role is unpinned elsewhere).
5. SCOPE RECONCILIATION with bead kernel-of-truth-hu10 (OPEN, Opus): hu10's define-lane scopes the kappa_B^engine census to Tier-A DEFINITIONAL benchmarks only (WiC + biomedical def-MMLU + OpenBookQA control) and requires the define-op mapper leg + C5 filter implemented FIRST (design-kot-query-define-op sec.7.1). b-cov-smol (all nine) is a strict superset. Should b-cov-smol proceed only after hu10's define-lane lands, or is a separate instrument intended?
6. (minor/moot until 1-4) per-benchmark sample n: split is pinned (sec.1.6 validation splits) but n is not; disclosed default (full split or 200 seed-pinned) is mechanical. TriviaQA licence now Apache-2.0 (reports/lit-eval-benchmarks.md) — confirm at freeze.

CORROBORATION (3 independent): poc/define-op-census/RUN-LOG.md boundary-stop #2 ("NOT buildable by adapting m0b"); bead hu10 OPEN framing kappa_B^engine as an instrument to be built (definitional-scope); design-kot-query-define-op sec.7.1 places mapper leg + C5 as implementation prerequisites before sec.7.2 measurement.

NEXT: route to Fable design wave after the current B'/registry writer frees (serialize — no concurrent Fable registry writer). Correction implied for benchmark-evaluation-strategy sec.4 (b-cov-smol is not the ~$0 no-gate mechanical first cut it is framed as).

title: Register .claude/agents/* as invocable subagent_types (SDK launch-config fix)
description: ROOT CAUSE (claude-code-guide investigation 2026-07-09): this environment runs the Agent SDK headless; custom .claude/agents/*.md files are NOT auto-discovered by the Agent tool, so subagent_type resolves only built-ins (claude/general-purpose/Explore/Plan/claude-code-guide/statusline-setup) and errors 'Agent type X not found'. FIX is at SESSION INITIALIZATION (outside the running session, so Kern cannot apply it): when launching the SDK session, EITHER set setting_sources/settingSources = ['user','project','local'] so .claude/ (incl. agents/) is loaded, AND/OR load each .claude/agents/*.md into an AgentDefinition and pass via the ClaudeAgentOptions 'agents' parameter. The agent files are correctly formatted (valid name/description/model frontmatter); they just aren't registered. VERIFY after: retry Agent with subagent_type='architecture-advisor' -> should resolve. Until fixed, workaround = spawn general-purpose with first line 'read and adopt .claude/agents/<role>.md' + short delta (works, but no cross-spawn system-prompt caching).
notes: CORRECTED ROOT CAUSE + FIX APPLIED (2026-07-09): NOT the SDK-agents-param theory. Kern is launched by Claude Code (interactive), whose PROJECT ROOT is /home/ec2-user/css (confirmed: /home/ec2-user/css/.claude/{settings.local.json,scheduled_tasks.lock} + memory slug -home-ec2-user-css). Claude Code scans <project-root>/.claude/agents/ + ~/.claude/agents/ — NOT arbitrary subdirs — but the 7 role files live at /home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/.claude/agents/ (a subdir), and neither scanned dir existed. FIX APPLIED: symlink /home/ec2-user/css/.claude/agents -> /home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/.claude/agents (kernel repo = source of truth). Takes effect on the NEXT Kern launch (this session's agent registry is fixed at launch; cannot hot-reload). VERIFY after relaunch: Agent subagent_type='architecture-advisor' should resolve. PERSISTENCE (box ephemeral, symlink is outside the repo): either (a) launch Claude Code with cwd/project = the kernel repo so its own .claude/agents/ is scanned, or (b) add the symlink creation to the box-init/launch script. Fallback if Claude Code won't follow the dir symlink: copy the .md files into /home/ec2-user/css/.claude/agents/.

title: Upgrade path: OBO genus-differentia + FrameNet frames -> profile-1 structured explications
description: The ontology-mining wave produced the only bulk sources with DEFINITIONAL (not merely taxonomic) structure, flagged upgradeCandidate in-record. TWO complementary upgrade paths, both to be prototyped: (1) OBO genus-differentia (9,303 GO logical defs: X = genus that rel filler) -> upgrade NOMINAL concepts to InstanceSchema/RelationalSchema explications; the genus becomes the superordinate, the differentia (property+filler) becomes a WhenTrue/RelationalSchema clause. (2) FrameNet frames (1,221, with 3,501 Core FEs) -> upgrade EVENT/RELATIONAL concepts to RelationalSchema explications; the Core FEs become the referent slots (valency pre-solved), the NSM body authored by hand. SUMO <=> axioms are definitional too but are full FOL (need semantic translation the programme forbids at bulk scale) - NOT clean upgrade candidates. Prototype: pick 5 GO defs + 5 FrameNet frames, hand-upgrade to kot-ast/1, measure how much of the explication the structured source supplies vs must be authored.

title: Bulk-mine further OBO Foundry ontologies (ChEBI subset, CL, PATO, PO, UBERON) into onto-obo
description: onto-obo v0.1 shipped BFO/RO/GO (39,012 records, 9,303 genus-differentia). The kot-obo/1 extractor is generic OBO 1.4 and re-usable as-is. Next high-value targets rich in intersection_of logical definitions: ChEBI (chemical genus-differentia; 270MB full - take a principled subset with the rule stated, e.g. is_a-closure of a top slice, since it exceeds a comfortable working set), CL (cell types), PATO (qualities - maps to profile-1 attributes), PO/UBERON (anatomy, targets of GO's part_of/occurs_in differentia). Each is CC BY / CC0 (verify per manifest). Pattern: add to ONTOLOGIES[] in data/onto-obo/extractor/extract.mjs with sha pin; owner-dedup already handles cross-imports.
notes:
Partial execution (Opus kb-pipeline-runner, 2026-07-09). DONE: appended PATO + PO to the deterministic ONTOLOGIES[] (generic OBO-1.4, 0 parser-logic changes). PATO 1,883 records (0 genus-differentia, CC BY 3.0, sha 9b65efdf...1f75); PO 1,670 records (81 genus-differentia, CC BY 4.0, sha d9094973...f4c9). onto-obo 39,012 -> 42,565 records. BFO/RO/GO byte-identical. Byte-reproducible (2x). validate.mjs green; parser tests 11/11; sample-review 0 err. Re-minted (--corpus onto-obo): 42,565 URNs, 0 dup groups. Committed caeb1a7 (push pending: blocked by concurrent lit-KB agent kb/ state at the shared pre-push honesty gate, not by onto-obo).

DEFERRED to a FABLE owner-dedup / emission-policy decision -- CL + UBERON. Both parse cleanly (0 throws) and are permissive (CL CC BY 4.0, UBERON CC BY 3.0), BUT the pinned first-declarer owner-dedup (PREFIX_OWNER={BFO,RO,GO}) mishandles their heavy cross-imports: CL declares 5,062 UBERON stub ids and UBERON declares 1,487 CL stub ids (mutual import -- no array order makes both correct), so ingesting as-is mis-owns 5,062 UBERON terms (genus-differentia defs replaced by empty stubs) and emits 3,852 foreign-prefix stub records (PR 1006, CHEBI 731 [out of scope], NCBITaxon 507, 492 raw http: IRIs, DHBA/MBA/CLM/NBO/...). Needs: (1) PREFIX_OWNER ownership-logic update + (2) a foreign-prefix-stub emission policy. Both are extractor-logic/curation changes outside the pipeline-runner boundary. ChEBI subset also still deferred (Fable curation, unchanged).

Counts: kernel-v0 54, math-v0 39, math-mm 2998, molecules-v0 54, physics-v0 83, physics-qudt 3070, lexical-wn31 117791, onto-obo 39012, onto-sumo 19300, onto-framenet 3291, haiku-tier 84 (live-stream snapshot). math-lean-sample unminted by signed design (design-lean-route.md). title: Wikipedia/Haiku pipeline stage 2: pilot en-masse (hundreds) + measured scale-out cost table for maintainer decision

comments.author: ec2-user
comments.text:
STAGE-2 READINESS (from stages 0+1, commit b787268; full evidence: data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md).

RECOMMENDATION: conditional GO for the pre-registered hundreds-scale pilot; NO-GO for unattended multi-week volume on today's numbers.

Ready: 56,628-lemma prioritised inventory (rank order = value order); cached fetcher (Wiktionary 100%); real-validator gates; modelAuthored schema (status below Explicated, full provenance incl. promptVersionHash + repairPromptVersionHash + pipelineVersionHash + source revisions); volume runner data/haiku-tier/runner/run-volume.py implementing the s1 winner (draft + gate-error-fed repair + normalization), with session governor (default 500 calls/5h window, concurrency <=2, usage-limit back-off with reset-time parsing, checkpointed, per-window usage log for maintainer visibility). Dry-run verified; zero volume calls made.

Measured basis (n=50): 26% of concepts -> gate-legal records at ~1.8 calls/concept (~7 calls/legal record); honest cannot-formalise 16%; passing records correct on inspection but sometimes thinned by repair; fidelity vs hand-authored 1.47/2 (agent-judged, single annotator).

Suggested pilot: 2 windows / ~1,000 calls ~= 550 band-A/B concepts -> expect ~140 records + ~90 abstentions. Pilot should ALSO measure: (1) a second repair round (untested; plausible 32%->45%+ of attempted for +0.8 calls/concept); (2) human or stronger-model review of a record sample before any coverage claims; (3) optional sonnet-draft arm if 26% yield is judged too low. Catalog note: expressible space grows with molecules-v0 — re-measure after the next molecule batch.

Throughput at governed rate: band A+B (3,601 concepts) ~= 13 windows ~= 4.5 days at 3 windows/day -> ~940 records; A+B+C (17,990) ~= 65 windows ~= 22 days; full inventory ~= 204 windows ~= 68 days. Wall-clock per window ~20 min of API activity; the plan-capacity share, not compute, binds.

Fidelity vs hand-authored 1.47/2 (agent-judged, 0/15 wrong). modelAuthored schema + promptVersionHash/pipelineVersionHash provenance emitted; session-governed volume runner ready NOT run. Full readout: data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md.

title: Ontology mining wave: OBO Foundry logical definitions (genus-differentia), SUMO KIF axioms, FrameNet frames+valency, DOLCE/gUFO — adapt into AxiomsOnly/structured tiers with provenance
close_reason:
Ontology-mining wave shipped 3 new bulk tiers (all deterministic, validated, sample-audited 0 err, unit-tested; licences verified + recorded per source):

onto-obo (BFO/RO/GO): 39,012 AxiomsOnly records; HEADLINE 9,303 GO genus-differentia logical definitions (X = genus that rel filler) as structured logicalDefinition axioms inside identity - a tier richer than WordNet hypernymy; upgradeCandidate-flagged. Licences: BFO CC BY 4.0, GO CC BY 4.0, RO CC0 - all REDISTRIBUTABLE. 150+756 sample audit 0 err; 11 unit tests.

onto-sumo (Merge+MILO): 15,595 canonical SUO-KIF axiom strings + 3,705 declared terms (2483 class/746 relation), 48 terms with <=> syntactic definitions. Licence: IEEE SUMO permissive - REDISTRIBUTABLE w/ attribution. Every kif a canonical fixed point (0 fails); 200 axiom+400 doc audit 0 err; 8 unit tests.

onto-framenet (FrameNet 1.7): 1,221 frames (3,501 Core FEs = valency skeleton) + 2,070 frame relations (12,393 FE maps). Licence: CC BY 3.0 (verified via NLTK index; zip sha matches) - REDISTRIBUTABLE w/ attribution. LICENSING BOUNDARY: official site gates download behind a registration form (courtesy tracker, not licence wall); used NLTK's authorized CC-BY copy, did not scrape around it, extracted only frame metadata not the annotated corpus. README has the 1-page valency->profile-1 mapping analysis.

Alignment bridges emitted for all 3 (BFO occurrent<->Event, part_of<->BFO:0000050/SUMO part, give<->Giving, etc.). Follow-ups filed: further OBO ontologies; genus-differentia + FrameNet-frames -> profile-1 explication upgrade paths (the only bulk sources with definitional structure); SUMO domain files; DOLCE/gUFO.

title: X3-corpus: 13/79 meaning-inverting edits closer than nearest distinct pair; deep-quote attenuation; no notBoost variant dominates
notes: kernel-v0 corpus numbers (poc/results/x3-kernel-v0-report.md, kernel-of-truth-138): polarity flips median cos 0.9974 (max 0.99992, 'afraid' — flip buried in a quote), NOT-wrap median 0.8752, while meaning-PRESERVING alpha-renames median 0.7036 — the ordering a meaning-faithful similarity needs is inverted. 13 of 79 inverting edits leave a concept closer to its own negation than afraid<->sad (cos 0.9933), the nearest distinct authored pair; offenders are quote-heavy concepts (afraid, liar, promise, wrong, trustworthy, right...) — same deep-quote attenuation mechanism as the X2-corpus decode failures (kernel-of-truth-0kn). notBoost=2/4 widens NOT-wrap only (0.75/0.53) but cannot touch pred-level polarity flips and degrades geometry (RDM Spearman 0.989/0.939) => still no dominating variant; similarity-consumer ban stands (architecture.md 1.2, panel O9). Candidate work: polarity-aware treatment at the predicate level (WANT/DON'T-WANT, GOOD/BAD codes), and depth/quote weight compensation.

Decoder: deep-quote SNR — within-clause local refinement (restore-self/peel-siblings inside decodeClause) to push exact recovery past the depth-4 x 8-clause boundary case (X2 719/720 in reduced run)
close_reason: Fixed in 0d21de1 (decoder-only). Root cause: decode-order SNR loss, not encoder capacity — unpeeled adjunct atoms + unpeeled sibling quote clause turned into noise on deep structured-child estimates (deep-chain corr 0.60->0.15), pushing the true atomic filler under the 5-sigma gate, after which the required-slot assumePresent path fabricated a phantom SP. Fix: two-phase atomic-first role sweep in decodeClause, deferred KIND/PART of-resolution in decodeSP, restore-self/peel-siblings refinement in decodeClauseList, and required-slot matched-filter competition (raw unbind scale) in decodeRole. X2 full grid 720/720 exact, pre-registered gate PASS; depth 5-6 also 100% with higher confidence. Encoder content-hash UNCHANGED (40e8c8ba...e2d10c); X0 goldens byte-identical; unit suite 31/31 incl. new regression test.

comments.author: ec2-user
comments.text: E8 harness READY-TO-RUN (run gated on coordinator spend approval). Design pinned in poc/e8/README.md BEFORE build. Sources (both ungated, HF-revision-pinned): gpt2 + jbloom/GPT2-Small-SAEs-Reformatted blocks.6.hook_resid_pre (L1 ReLU, d_sae 24576, 151MB) and EleutherAI/pythia-160m + sae-pythia-160m-32k layers.5 (TopK k=32, 32768 latents, 201MB); both = residual between blocks 5/6 = hidden_states[6] = L/2. Gemma Scope rejected for primary pair (base model gated). Item set = 51 E2 analysis items; molecules EXCLUDED (no derived vectors; task premise 54+108 corrected to 54+54 in README). Operationalisation: mean per-token SAE-feature activation over E2 context banks -> within-family cosine RDMs -> cross-family correspondence X via masked profile-Spearman; primary P1 = Mantel/shuffled-kernel on K_jl512 vs X_sym p<0.01, co-primary P2 = partial | orig3; kernel-free gate G first. Controls all green: planted-correspondence PASS, structured-null NULL (gate passes, kernel signal absent), pure-noise PRECONDITION FAIL; mock e2e + stubbed-Modal wiring green (bash poc/e8/validate.sh). RUN: poc/modal/.venv/bin/modal run poc/modal/modal_e8.py (T4, ~1.28GB to kot-hf-cache volume, est $0.06-0.19 cold / $0.03-0.10 warm; envelope was $5-15) then python3 poc/e8/analyze.py poc/e8/results-incoming/<stamp>-modal (~25 min niced CPU). Weakest link (pinned, README §6): the concept->feature mapping routes through the exponent WORD in synthetic contexts — the SAE never sees the explication; orig3/emb4 partials are the only guard against word-relatedness carrying the result.

description: Deferred from F1K-BRINGUP-GATE-FIX.md REV-C (fork decision: wrapper-level enforcement chosen; builder untouched). title: Fable: g3-llmproxy-v3 — apply truthstyle A1 vendor-id-tail exclusion to the §6 blinding scan
description: g3-v2 run-aborted at pass-A item 61 on a §6 blinding FALSE POSITIVE: nsM inside a CLI request_id (req_011...EfnsM) in events.jsonl. SAME class as truthstyle p3, which Fable already solved via Amendment A1 (exclude tokens inside the base62 tail of (req|msg)_ vendor-id literals on harness-captured surfaces). g3's §6 blinding contract greps the WHOLE events.jsonl and never inherited A1. Fix = v3 re-stage applying the A1 rule (or restrict the scan to judge-emitted text / user-prompt bytes) — judge-invocation.md is sha-pinned in the frozen v2 record so it needs a new record. LOWER PRIORITY: g3 is a WEAK quarantined proxy (human g3 is the gold gate; g9 FAIL + m0a PASS already give the proxy picture); 3rd g3 boundary stop (v1 fence, v2 blinding). Decide with maintainer whether the g3 proxy signal is worth a 3rd attempt. Fence fix (v2) also still unexercised at pass-B scale.

title: A-E2 external-data fetch decision (frequency corpora + host tokenizers)
description: A-E2 (idea A cross-lingual savings census) is blocked on external downloads the Tier-0 $0/no-fetch rule forbids without authorization: pinned per-language frequency lists (wordfreq/Leipzig/OSCAR/CC-100 — source+hash pin decision), host tokenizers (SmolLM2-135M tokenizer.json + an R4 tokenizer + a JS/py BPE evaluator), and pilot-language surface data (A-F1 alignment fork). Blocker fully specified: poc/compression-census/results/a-e2-blocker.json. Gated on the Fable interpretation of B-E0 (if idea B/A deprioritized, may not be worth fetching).

title: Fable: define-op per-concept DISENDORSE wire format
description: define-op impl (bead 46f) boundary-stopped: the memo (§3/ASM-DEF-3) says 'per-concept override retained' but does not specify the wire format for DISENDORSING a single contested definition. The corpus-scoped 'definitional' endorsement is implemented + tested; the disendorse mechanism is left unimplemented pending this design choice. Low urgency (no mandated test needs it).

title: ConceptNet -> CommonsenseQA world-layer pilot (Wave C, world-layer-gated)
description: Per 7fm Wave C (HOLD/design): ConceptNet is the CommonsenseQA kappa_B lever but needs the lookup-op + a world-layer pipeline (world-v0 is only 598 records; no external-DB->world pipeline exists). Design the ConceptNet->world-layer ingestion + the lookup-op grammar path. Gated behind define-op + subClassOf-op (ERR_AXIOM_UNIMPLEMENTED).

title: Revisit confirmatory logged-result reuse ONLY if GPU spend becomes material
description: DEFERRED 2026-07-09 after 2 Codex audits (audit-2 REFUTE, 4 criticals). Confirmatory reuse via reused_from is fail-closed. Reopen ONLY if GPU spend stops being ~$1/run (e.g. expensive frontier/multi-hour runs make the ~$1/run saving material). A revision-2 owes the 6 remaining amendments in registry/audits/resource-optimization-plan/2-design-reaudit-codex.json (RC-7 real pre-unblinding-priority proof; RC-4 primary-depends-on-fresh-arm; Case-A non-final-row refusal; verdict-time RC-2 model-proof; ratification plan_path binding; the missing refusal-path tests) before any re-audit. Fable owns the redesign.

title: Decide: molecule/haiku grounding-ref substitution vs stable placeholder ids (generation 2)
description: Generation-1 minting kept molecules-v0 and haiku-tier in stable-ref mode: grounding refs (mostly cross-corpus into kernel-v0, embedded in authored prose as {urn:...|gloss} tokens) were NOT rewritten to minted urn:kot: URNs. Rationale: task rule 'cross-corpus refs stay annotations/never rewritten into payloads' + do-not-alter-authored-bytes + the gist s3.5 1024-byte NFC grounding-note cap (substitution grows notes ~48 bytes/ref). Tension: gist s3.5 rule 4 wants grounding refs to be FINAL URNs inside the hash. Decide before generation 2; if substitution wins, specify the cap semantics on the resolved form and re-mint both corpora.

title: Bulk-corpus READMEs: document the minted identity surface (sourceId INSIDE identity)
description: The mint pass (kernel-of-truth-mud) put the stable sourceId INSIDE every bulk-extracted corpus's identity payload — required empirically: with the lexical-wn31 README's literal 'inside identity' list ({schema, semanticStatus, pos, ssType, axioms}, no offset), 66,569 of 117,791 synsets collapse to shared URNs (one group of 3,620 axiom-less adjectives) — an unsound merge of distinct concepts. The manifests' minting.identityPayload blocks document the actual splits; the READMEs of lexical-wn31 (and the other bulk corpora) should be updated to match, citing design-bulk-kernel.md ('a bulk record is an assertion by an extractor about a source').

title: E8 feature-level correspondence variant (independent corpus)
description: poc/e8 tests CONCEPT-level correspondence; a feature-level test through the same 51 concept probes is degenerate (target and predictor are functions of the same activation profiles — README §6.4). Non-circular version: establish cross-family FEATURE correspondence on an independent shared text corpus (e.g. a fixed 10-50k-sentence slice, activation-profile correlation, the universality-literature method), then test whether kernel coordinates of concept-anchored features predict which features correspond. Bigger extraction (~GPU-hours not minutes); design doc first.

title: E8 third family: Gemma Scope (gated) for a second family-pair
description: poc/e8 primary pair is gpt2 + pythia-160m (both ungated). Gemma Scope (google/gemma-scope-2b-pt-res, ungated SAEs; suggest layer 12 width 16k canonical-L0 params.npz, JumpReLU) is the flagship suite and the natural third family, but base model google/gemma-2-2b is gated=manual: needs coordinator HF account licence acceptance + HF_TOKEN as a Modal secret. A second pair would let E8 report a >=2-of-N quantifier instead of the single-pair scope limit (poc/e8/README.md §6.2). JumpReLU encode = act * (act > threshold) with params.npz keys W_enc/b_enc/W_dec/b_dec/threshold — add arch 'gemma_jumprelu' to e8_runner.SAEDict.

title: E8 molecule-tier extension: blocked on molecule-vector derivation
description: poc/e8 runs on the 51 explicated kernel-v0 analysis items only. molecules-v0 (54 records) have no derived kernel vectors — grounding notes without explications; docs/design-molecule-tier.md pins molecule-vector derivation (codebook-style from the grounding note) as an encoder version change => new content hash => new pre-registration. Once derived: extend poc/e8/build_inputs.py item set + author bank assignments for molecule words (mostly noun bank; judgement to be pinned), and re-run. Depends conceptually on the encoder follow-up named in design-molecule-tier.md.

title: onto-sumo: extract SUMO domain .kif files beyond Merge+MILO
description: onto-sumo v0.1 extracted the definitional core (Merge.kif + Mid-level-ontology.kif, 15,595 axioms). SUMO has ~100 domain .kif files (Cars, Hotel, Medicine, Geography, ...) at github.com/ontologyportal/sumo. The kot-sumo/1 KIF parser+extractor handles them unchanged (add files to FILES[] with sha pins; owner/subject logic is file-agnostic). Domain files add term coverage but are less definitional (mostly instance/subclass facts). Do as demand warrants; note the one dangling target (FabricIron -> HouseUtilityAppliance) resolves once HouseholdAppliances.kif is added.

title: Assess + mine DOLCE / gUFO (foundational ontologies) into an onto-dolce tier
description: The claim bead title also named DOLCE/gUFO; the mining directive scoped OBO/SUMO/FrameNet, so DOLCE/gUFO deferred here. gUFO (github.com/nemo-ufes/gufo) is directly relevant: kernel-v0 already targets gufo:Event and gufo:Kind, and onto-obo's alignment bridges to BFO (gUFO-adjacent). gUFO is OWL (Turtle) - would need a small Turtle/OWL reader (the programme avoided hand-rolled RDF/XML for BFO; Turtle is more tractable). DOLCE (DOLCE-Lite OWL, or the Loux/CLIF form) is the other classic upper ontology. Value: sharpen the continuant/occurrent + endurant/perdurant + rigid-sortal (Kind) alignments that kernel-v0's gUFO commitments depend on. Check licences (gUFO CC BY; DOLCE per ISTC-CNR terms).

title: Physics: report physics-qudt audit findings upstream to qudt/qudt-public-repo — 72 adjudicated discrepancies incl. faraday CODATA-2002 value, pre-2012 au, momme 1000x, 8 zero multipliers, AC-FT_US truncated-intermediate (qudt-discrepancies.md); consolidate + throttle per upstream-contribution etiquette; coordinate with @jeswr before filing

Physics: ISO 80000 naming/dimension alignment for physics-qudt kinds — QUDT mirrored faithfully incl. questionable stamps (MagneticPolarization A/m vs tesla, MacroscopicCrossSection L2 vs 1/m, MagneticReluctivity E1 vs E2 — qudt-discrepancies.md §4); align labels/dims against ISO 80000 as annotations, never silent fixes

Physics: rational-exponent dimension vectors — QUDT has 2 fractional qkdv vectors + MotorConstant/N-M-PER-W0dot5 stamped with WRONG integer vectors (qudt-discrepancies.md §1.8); extend Z^7 to Q^7 under the same exact-arithmetic discipline

Physics: log-scale unit record extension (dB, Np, pH, weighted decibels) — non-affine maps need their own exact base/reference semantics; named out of v0 and physics-qudt scope (design-physics-sector.md §4.3; 17 units excluded)

Physics: information-units normal form (bit family) — QUDT normalises to nats via ln 2; decide a bit-coherent exact normal form or a symbolic-log scalar extension; 50 units excluded pending this (data/physics-qudt/exclusions.json transcendental-factor + blocked)
math-mm: grammar-parse dependency refinement
description: Current dependency semantics is token-level (stated rule in data/math-mm/extractor/extract.mjs header + README). Documented precision limits found in the 100-record review: (1) punctuation attribution - '(' -> wi, ',' -> wif, braces -> cab; (2) pattern-reuse formers (csn, cpr, w3o, cv, ...) invisible inside definientia (e.g. df-tp lacks a df-sn dep); (3) cv unreachable. Fix requires parsing each statement against set.mm's (unambiguous) grammar to get parse-tree deps. Deterministic, self-contained upgrade; re-extraction discipline applies (new extractorHash, re-run all gates).

title: math-mm: mint content-addressed ids (RDF record form for pm-mm/1 + concept-hash pipeline over the 2998 records)
description: data/math-mm records carry set.mm-label placeholder URNs (urn:math-mm:<label>). Design-math-sector.md SS3.5 specifies how profile-M plugs into the gist SS6 hash machinery; pm-mm/1 needs its RDF record form + profile bundle header before real urn:concept: ids can be minted. Blocker for cross-profile references.

title: lexical-wn31: sense-frequency weighting as annotations (cntlist/index.sense tag counts)
description: Source dict ships cntlist + index.sense tag_cnt (SemCor). Attach per-lemma-sense frequency annotations (OUTSIDE identity) to support frequency-aware alignment + a weighted reachability curve. See data/lexical-wn31/alignment-kernel-v0.json method note.

title: lexical-wn31: adjective cluster structure (head/satellite + pertainym/participle/attribute)
description: similarTo is extracted; the rest of the adj cluster machinery (pertainym \\, participle <, attribute =, also-see ^) is not. Decide which are honest structural axioms vs annotations, extract, regen. See data/lexical-wn31/README.md.

title: lexical-wn31: extract verb-frame axioms (sentence frames + frame pointers)
description: Parser already parses f_cnt/frames from data.verb (parse.mjs) but extract.mjs drops them. Add typed frame axioms (or a frames block inside identity) + regen shards. Also consider verb-group ($) pointers. See data/lexical-wn31/README.md follow-ups list.

title: Bridge annotations: profile-M (math-v0) <-> Mathlib names
description: Per docs/design-lean-route.md §2/§6.2. Design the sameConceptAs-style annotation record shape once (signed, annotation-layer, per-link confidence + honesty notes), then link the 39 data/math-v0 records to (mathlibCommit,name) anchors where honest counterparts exist (e.g. addition~Nat.add, less-or-equal~Nat.le, divides~Nat.dvd variants...). Shape should be reusable for the future Metamath<->Lean crosswalk. Unblocked; small. Sample anchors available in data/math-lean-sample/records.jsonl.

title: kernel-v0 ontology follow-up: concepts inside/near duplicate primes INSIDE/NEAR (sense-identical in 50/50 sampled uses each) and label 'kind' collides with dominant English 'nice' sense (49/50) — consider rename/alias/drop at the next kernel version bump (touches pre-reg-pinned manifest = new pre-registration, NOT an amendment; measured alias frequencies in m0a-shadowed-policy.md section 2b)
Decoder residual completion is insertion-only: extend the global matched-filter competition to SUBSTITUTION/REMOVAL of wrong optional content
notes: The kernel-of-truth-0kn fix recovers MISSED optional content from the global residual (v minus re-encoded decode) via insertion candidates accepted only when the re-encoded edit explains the restored residual strictly better. ok-but-wrong decodes where content was decoded WRONG (wrong atom/quant/phantom optional slot) leave a +true/-decoded signature in the same residual and are amenable to the same acceptance test with substitution/removal candidate generation (probe filled optional sites for a strictly-better replacement, incl. dropping the filler). No known current failure needs it (X2-corpus 54/54 target after 0kn) — this is robustness for future corpora.

title: kernel-v0: nearest pair is the emotion trio (afraid<->sad 0.116 rad), not the deliberately-near give/take/gift cluster
notes: X1-corpus (poc/results/x1-kernel-v0-report.md, kernel-of-truth-138): all-pairs minimum is afraid<->sad at 0.1156 rad (cos 0.9933), then happy<->sad 0.2051, afraid<->happy 0.2238; the authored-to-be-near possession-transfer cluster first appears at rank 4 (repair<->take 0.4884) and give/take/gift themselves are not in the top 10 at all (top10 in the report json). The emotion trio shares nearly all clause structure (feel + quote re-anchoring), differing only in small deep-quote fragments that the encoder attenuates. Implication for corpus authoring: structural overlap dominates geometric proximity, not intended semantic nearness — worth a corpus README note and maybe a v0.2 authoring pass if margin work wants give/take to be the stress pair.
